draft.introduction: Habakkuk 3 is a theophanic prayer-hymn describing God’s awesome appearance in cosmic warfare, ending with a remarkable declaration of faith regardless of circumstances. The prophet will rejoice in God even when the harvest fails.

translation.text: Though the fig tree should not blossom, nor fruit be on the vines, the produce of the olive fail and the fields yield no food, the flock be cut off from the fold and there be no herd in the stalls, yet I will rejoice in the Lord; I will take joy in the God of my salvation. God, the Lord, is my strength; he makes my feet like the deer’s.
translation.original_text: διότι συκῆ οὐ καρποφορήσει καὶ οὐκ ἔσται γενήματα ἐν ταῖς ἀμπέλοις

translator_notes.note: This confession of faith in total deprivation is considered one of the highest expressions of unconditional trust in the Hebrew Bible. It refuses to make faith contingent on blessing, anticipating Job’s “though he slay me, yet will I trust him.”
